song.title: The Old Rugged Cross
song.lyrics:
[V1] 1) On a hill far away Stood an old rugged cross, The emblem of suff’ring and shame,|| And I love that old cross Where the Dearest and Best For a world of lost sinners was slain.
[C] Chorus So I’ll cherish the old rugged cross, Till my trophies at last I lay down; I will cling to the old rugged cross, And exchange it some day for a crown.
[V2] 2) Oh, that old rugged cross, So despised by the world, Has a wondrous attraction for me;|| For the dear Lamb of God Left His glory above, To bear it to dark Calvary.
[V3] 3) In the old rugged cross, Stained with blood so divine, A wondrous beauty I see;|| For ’twas on that old cross Jesus suffered and died, To pardon and sanctify me.
song.author: George Bennard
